decisions.summary: Adopt issue Option 1 (preferred): stop persisting agent.output_received durably; emit it stream-only via eventStore.emitOnly() instead of appendAsync(). Rejected Option 2 (per-type shortened retention) — it keeps the write churn.

Reuse existing primitives: emitOnly (event-store.ts:304) for stream-only delivery and purgeType (event-store.ts:334) for the one-time cleanup of ~264k existing rows, mirroring the issues.snapshot purge already in initEventStore (event-store.ts:378).

Reclaim freed disk via PRAGMA incremental_vacuum immediately after the one-time purge (only when rows were purged). Node production DB already has auto_vacuum=INCREMENTAL (src/lib/database/index.ts:96); the 15-min periodic reclaimer is the backstop. Do not add a second full VACUUM.

Keep agent.output_received in the public SSE catalog as LIVE-ONLY (not replayable). Fix formatFrame in routes/events.ts to omit the SSE id: line for sequence < 0 so emitOnly frames never poison a consumer's Last-Event-ID resume cursor. Update docs/EXTERNAL-EVENT-STREAM.md accordingly.

Do NOT add agent.output_received to the readFromStmt exclusion list (event-store.ts:187). After emitOnly + purge there are no rows to exclude; that would be downstream defensive code against a purge-failed scenario, which the no-bandaids rule forbids.

hazards.summary: Live UI must keep working after the switch. Risk: a work agent deletes the event emission entirely instead of switching to emitOnly, breaking the dashboard god view / RPC subscribeAgentOutput.
hazards.mitigation: MUST use eventStore.emitOnly(event). Verified: streamEvents (domain-services.ts:280) is backed by store.subscribe(), so emitOnly reaches subscribeAgentOutput (ws-rpc.ts:627), subscribeIssueEvents, and the SSE live tail. The producer test asserts emitOnly is called with the correct event.

hazards.summary: emitOnly events carry sequence -1. Risk of poisoning SSE Last-Event-ID resume for any unfiltered consumer (since-guard requires since >= 0).
hazards.mitigation: formatFrame must omit the id: line when sequence < 0. AC covers both branches. Persisted rows are AUTOINCREMENT >= 1, so this can never affect real-event resume.

hazards.summary: Frontend gap/resync coordinator could spam replay on the high-frequency sequence -1 stream (every 3s x every agent).
hazards.mitigation: Already safe: recoveryCoordinator.classifyDomainEvent (recoveryCoordinator.ts:72-75) returns 'apply' for sequence === -1 with no replay; reducer uses Math.max(state.sequence, -1) (no-op). issues.snapshot already exercises this path. No frontend change needed.

hazards.summary: Immediate disk reclaim is runtime-dependent. Under Bun (dev only) auto_vacuum is not set on the event-store's own connection, so incremental_vacuum is a no-op; production is Node 22 only.
hazards.mitigation: Phrase the reclaim AC for the Node production path (freelist returns to OS). The unit test verifies the PURGE (rows -> 0), not the VACUUM, to avoid fragile auto_vacuum-mode test scaffolding.

hazards.summary: No secondary bloat source. mapDomainEventToDetailed (domain-services.ts:188) auto-emits activity.detailed for many event types.
hazards.mitigation: Verified there is NO agent.output_received case in mapDomainEventToDetailed (returns null) — switching to emitOnly does not strand a secondary persisted activity.detailed stream. No change needed.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1925: perf(dashboard): events table ~1 GB — agent.output_received (721 MB of terminal output) persisted durably

Auto-planning complete: 3-bead plan (emitOnly switch + one-time purge/reclaim + SSE Last-Event-ID safety). Option 1 chosen. All consumers traced; emitOnly path verified end-to-end (server stream, RPC, SSE, frontend coordinator, reducer).

1. Read the error output above carefully
2. Fix the code causing the failure
3. Run the failing check locally to verify it passes
4. Commit every change
5. Invoke the /rebase-and-submit skill for PAN-1925 — this is an atomic task. Because verification already ran once (a PR exists), the skill will push your branch and run `pan review request PAN-1925 -m "Fixed build"` for you. NEVER curl `/api/review/...` or any dashboard endpoint — `pan review request` is the only supported re-entry point.

Do NOT stop between steps. Do NOT stop after pushing. Do NOT stop until `pan review request` has completed successfully.
